title: clematis alpina subsp. ochotensis
intro: Clematis alpina subsp. ochotensis, also known as the Siberian clematis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, native to eastern Russia and northern China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid content. The health benefits of clematis alpina subsp. ochotensis include its ability to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
